title: BC-06 — Identity & Access
description: Data contract for the Identity & Access Bounded Context. Defines the User aggregate with its Role, RefreshToken, and PasswordResetToken lifecycle. Covers authentication (HU-01), password recovery (HU-02), password change (HU-28), and RBAC enforcement (QA-1, QA-2).

User.description: Aggregate Root. System access account. Stores credentials and activation status. Serves as the authentication identity for all system actors (HU-01).

email.description: Unique login identifier. Used for authentication and password recovery.

passwordHash.description: BCrypt-hashed password. Never exposed in API responses. Work factor configurable via env var (default 12).

active.description: Whether the account is active. Inactive accounts cannot authenticate.

refreshTokens.description: Active refresh tokens for this user. Supports concurrent sessions with device tracking.

passwordResetToken.description: Current password reset token, if any. At most one active at a time.

Role.description: Value Object. User type assigned to an account. Determines menu options and visible permissions after login (QA-1).

RoleType.description: Enum of system roles. Hierarchy: SYSTEM_ADMIN > COORDINATOR > ASSISTANT > PROFESSOR > STUDENT > SPEAKER.

RefreshToken.description: Entity within User aggregate. Persisted refresh token storing only the SHA-256 hash. Supports 'Remember me' via configurable TTL and concurrent session awareness via device tracking.

tokenHash.description: SHA-256 hash of the opaque refresh token. The plaintext token is never persisted.

expiresAt.description: Expiration timestamp. Default 7 days; 30 days for remember-me.

revoked.description: Whether this token has been explicitly revoked (logout or password change).

deviceInfo.description: User-Agent or device identifier for concurrent session awareness.

PasswordResetToken.description: Value Object. Time-limited token for password recovery flow (HU-02). SHA-256 hashed, 30 min TTL, single-use. Lifecycle: create() → isExpired() → isUsed() → markAsUsed().

tokenHash.description: SHA-256 hash of the reset token sent via email.

expiresAt.description: Expiration timestamp. Default 30 minutes from creation.

used.description: Single-use flag. Once used, the token cannot be reused.

LoginCommand.description: Command to authenticate a user. Produces a JWT access token and a refresh token on success (HU-01).

rememberMe.description: If true, refresh token TTL is extended to 30 days.

RefreshTokenCommand.description: Command to exchange a valid refresh token for a new access token.

refreshToken.description: Opaque refresh token (plaintext). Server hashes it and looks up the stored hash.

LogoutCommand.description: Command to revoke a specific refresh token (logout from one device).

ForgotPasswordCommand.description: Command to request a password reset email (HU-02). Always returns success to prevent email enumeration.

ResetPasswordCommand.description: Command to reset a password using a valid token received via email (HU-02).

token.description: Plaintext token from the reset email. Server hashes it for lookup.

newPassword.description: New password. Must meet complexity requirements.

ChangePasswordCommand.description: Command to change a user's password (HU-28). Self-change requires current password. Coordinator-change skips it.

userId.description: Target user. For self-change, must match the authenticated user.

currentPassword.description: Required for self-change. Not required when coordinator changes another user's password.

AuthResponse.description: Read model returned after successful login or token refresh.

accessToken.description: JWT signed with RSA-256. Contains userId, role, graduateProgramId. TTL: 15 min.

refreshToken.description: Opaque token. Sent via HttpOnly cookie in production.

JwtClaims.description: Claims embedded in the JWT access token. Used by all downstream BCs for RBAC and multi-tenant context.

graduateProgramId.description: Multi-tenant context. Scopes all downstream queries.

exp.description: Expiration — Unix timestamp. Default iat + 900 (15 min).
